articles.title: Uncle Sam is wrong, India and China are doing their bit to fight climate change
articles.summary: It is true that there is an increase in carbon emission by India and China due to homegrown consumption and lack of infrastructure. But let s keep in mind that India s carbon emissions per capita are still 10 times lower and China s is half of that compared to the US.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.summary: Indian stock indices Sensex and Nifty 50 rose on Thursday, boosted by falling crude oil prices after US-Iran negotiations. Nifty 50 gained 0.23%, while Sensex increased by 0.21%. Continued sideways trading seen, with key resistance at 24,100-24,200 and support at 23,850-23,800
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Adani Energy Solutions approves ₹10,000 crore fundraising via QIP; check Q1 results date for the Adani Group stock
articles.summary: Adani Energy Solutions plans to raise up to ₹10,000 crore via Qualified Institutional Placement, pending shareholder approval. The company will hold an Extraordinary General Meeting on July 25, 2026, to discuss the proposal. Its stock performance shows significant growth over the past year.

articles.title: Tight funding norms for prop traders may hit expiry day volumes

articles.summary: Gold and silver prices traded mixed in the domestic futures market on Thursday morning, amid largely positive global cues. The dollar index remains above 101, keeping bullion expensive for buyers in overseas currencies.

articles.title: Rupee opens 32 paise higher at 94.93 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee opened 32 paise stronger at 94.93 against the US dollar, influenced by falling crude prices. Traders expect limited recovery due to weaknesses in other Asian currencies and rising US Treasury yields, with the RBI likely to manage currency volatility.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Gold, silver prices today: Comex gains as softer US jobs data, Warsh remarks ease Fed hike bets
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices rose on July 1 after a poor quarterly performance, aided by easing fears of a US rate hike and a weaker dollar. Despite recent volatility, markets are pricing a 65% chance of a September rate hike as employment data falls short of expectations.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Meta Platforms surges 12% on report of AI cloud service plans to rival Amazon, Microsoft
articles.summary: Meta Platforms' shares rose nearly 12% after reports of launching a cloud computing business aimed at selling AI services. The initiative, part of Meta Compute, could position the company against Amazon and Microsoft in the AI infrastructure market.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Adani Green Energy to announce June quarter results on this date. Check details
articles.summary: Adani Green Energy's Board will meet on July 22, 2026, to approve financial results for Q1FY27. For the March ending quarter, the company reported a 34% year-on-year (YoY) increase in consolidated net profit to ₹514 crore
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Indian Bank Q1 business update: Lender reports 13.3% growth in deposits, advances rise 13.9%
articles.summary: Indian Bank reported a 13.6% YoY growth in total business to ₹15.28 lakh crore for Q1 FY26. Deposits rose 13.3% and gross advances increased 13.9%. Net profit for the quarter reached ₹3,103 crore, while asset quality improved with GNPA and NNPA ratios declining.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Multibagger Paisalo Digital hits 20% upper circuit as promoters raise stake to 46.72%
articles.summary: Paisalo Digital's shares hit a 20% upper circuit at ₹71 after promoters increased their stake to 46.72%. This marks a steady rise in shareholding from 26% in FY19, showcasing long-term confidence and a strategy for doubling assets, income, and profit within three years.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Gold price drops below $4,000, down 12% in June - biggest monthly decline since 2008: what lies ahead?
articles.summary: Gold has fallen below the critical $4,000 level, sliding to around $3,960. Analysts predict potential rebounds due to oversold conditions, while long-term fundamentals remain supportive despite ongoing volatility tied to US interest rates and geopolitical factors.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Top Gainers & losers on 1 July: Reliance Power, Delhivery, Eternal, Paytm, MCX, Dabur India among top gainers
articles.summary: The Indian stock market opened positively in July, led by strong buying in realty, FMCG, and auto stocks, with Nifty 50 at 24,005 and Sensex at 76,873. However, IT and metal sectors saw declines as geopolitical tensions impacted crude oil prices.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Sensex rises over 400 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,000; investors earn over ₹2 lakh crore in a day
articles.summary: Equity benchmark Sensex clocked a gain of 444 points, or 0.58%, to close at 76,922.64, while the Nifty 50 settled 140 points, or 0.59%, higher at 24,005.85.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Japanese Yen sinks to weakest level since 1986: 3 reasons behind the historic slide
articles.summary: The yen has reached its lowest point against the US dollar in almost 40 years, prompting worries of intervention from Japanese authorities. The decline is fueled by a strong dollar, expectations of US rate hikes, and growing bearish bets on the yen, despite Japan's recent economic improvements.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Aurobindo Pharma’s investors await next leg of growth
articles.summary: Growth would be aided by the Pen-G project ramp-up, Lannett’s integration, new US product launches, expansion in Europe and contribution from higher-margin businesses
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Infosys shares slip below ₹1,000 for first time since 2020 as IT rout deepens; m-cap falls to ₹4 lakh crore
articles.summary: Infosys shares have dropped 40% this year, falling below ₹1,000 for the first time since September 2020. Contributing factors include AI disruption fears, weak earnings, and rising interest rates.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: 5kg LPG cylinder demand from immigrant labourers plummets up to 80% amid price surge
articles.summary: Demand for these portable gas cylinders creatively named after kids plunged after prices doubled following the West Asia war, at least four distributors said. These 5kg cylinders are primarily used by migrant workers and students, and can be bought off the shelf with just Aadhar as identity proof.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: The ‘Hi, hello’ monsoon: In rural India, an anxious, high-stakes wait for the skies to swell
articles.summary: A teasing sky is chocking central India’s fields and threatening harvests worth millions. Some Basmati growers and tribal farmers now face a brutal choice: wait for the rain, or pack for the factory floor.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Manufacturing PMI slows to 54.2 in June on softer demand
articles.summary: It was the second-slowest expansion in four years 2022, better than only March, the first month of West Asia war.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India's richest cities: urban affairs ministry is on a quest to assess economic output of cities for first time
articles.summary: The Indian government is creating a standardized methodology to estimate the economic output of cities, known as City Economic Product. The initiative aims to quantify urban economic contributions, aiding in planning and investment, as urban areas contribute nearly 70% of India's economic output.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: GST collections rise 14% to ₹1.95 trillion in June, as tax revenue from imports soars
articles.summary: June GST collections suggest economic activity remained resilient despite global uncertainties, with higher imports and steady domestic transactions supporting tax revenues.
articles.label: BULLISH
